content: Same problem. I am using FCM. Dont want to downgrade

I was getting the same problem on stable but it went away when I switched to the beta channel (1.22.0-12.1.pre). So something in those updates must have fixed it. If anybody else could test that and confirm it goes away after upgrading?

Edit: And it's still breaking :/. Was nice while it lasted.

It was breaking my mind and since I upgraded flutter to beta mode everything worked as expected.
Hope the next stable update fixes the problem.
(Was something truly _fixed_, or was the message simply _suppressed_?) 🤔
@awhitford The message keeps popping up, but my FCM plugins started working.
I still get messages after upgrading from 1.20.0-7.2.pre to 1.22.0-12.1.pre although somewhat less than before...
I am still seeing this after upgrading to Flutter 1.22, running against iPhone simulator.  (Admittedly, this does not seem very serious, but I honestly have no idea.)

same issue... any solution?
same issue

@HasithaPriyasad 

This issue doesn't prevent the app from running. As far as I can tell it doesn't actually effect the app at all, it's just an annoying error we can't get rid of. Your issue might be something else or closely related. Can you go into some more detail or if you feel it's different enough open another ticket for it?

I'd rather see you exercise that web support. 😉

Sorry to sidetrack but this may help some people: If you landed here because you searched for
`Error connecting to the service protocol: _flutter.listViews: (-32000) Bad state: The client closed with pending request "_flutter.listViews".`
You probably have an uncaught exception in your `configureFlutterEngine` function.

Exactly the same configuration as you, also encountered this problem.

I also had this issue, however in my case it was because my info.plist was malformed.
I changed the UILaunchStoryboardName to Main to avoid the IOS splash screen, changing this back to launchscreen fixed this.
```
<key>UILaunchStoryboardName</key>
<string>Main</string>
```
to 
```
<key>UILaunchStoryboardName</key>
<string>LaunchScreen</string>
```

Could everyone who still has this problem please file a new issue with the exact description of what happens, logs, and the output of `flutter doctor -v`.
All system setups can be slightly different, so it's always better to open new issues and reference related issues.

It looks like this commit should solve the issue: https://github.com/flutter/engine/commit/f81bc371c09f6565a0e7c420732666bc593ac0b5

However, I am unable to tell its status.  I don't think any of us are running this new code yet.

Yep. now it's completely unresponsive in the UI, and banging Q to quit the debugger hangs. Awesome; fire pan to fire. 

Editorial: So much for catching up on lost productivity today because of ferreting out other Flutter related issues all week. This platform is getting more fragile as time goes on. :(  I've been doing this a very long time, and I've seen many of these platforms and "operating environments" start off with a great idea and slowly fade (sometimes not so slow and more of a crash and burn than a fade) due to sloppy support :( I hope Flutter gets back on track. If we all bought our dev tools like the "old days", there'd be a commercially viable interested in ensuring the tools would be more solid and reliable. Borland Pascal, Borland C++, Microsoft Visual Studio 6.0, Visual Basic 3.0 - these were rock solid products. (sigh)

Hi, this problem has been fixed on never versions of the Flutter engine, one quick way to see that you are still on a older version of engine is that if the error happens on line 184 as opposed to 203. (see: https://github.com/flutter/engine/pull/22170/files). Can you see if this problem occurs on master channel? Thanks.

203 as opposed to 184 as seen in `[VERBOSE-2:profiler_metrics_ios.mm(184)] `

There isn't an easy way for me to reproduce this error and the linked change tried to pre-emptively handle this.
I'm on Flutter 1.22.4, Dart 2.10.4, seeing it at 184.

Updated my original comment: if you see this error on line 203 that means that this issue needs to be re-opened. If it happens on line: 184, you are on an older version of Flutter, please try the master channel and see if this is still a problem there. Thanks.

Please re-read my whole post. I did.

Seems like the older version of engine is being used still, given the errors. I'm not sure what is going on. I noticed that you did do a `flutter clean` and then `flutter run` which I would expect would have cleared any caches by the flutter tool and the new `flutter run` would've linked the project to the latest engine.

To me it still looks like the stable engine is being used. I'll reopen this issue.

Cool, thanks. Happy to help with any future specific-version testing, logs, etc.

No idea if this helps or how it's related, but I was running a new version of XCode and handn't installed the CLI utils yet.  After running `xcode-select --install` and restarting the simulator, I stopped receiving the error message.
